channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gauteng NGOs Face Funding Delays, Straining Essential Services
item.description: Funding delays in Gauteng have left hundreds of NGO workers unpaid and thousands of vulnerable individuals without critical services, according to the Gauteng Care Crisis Committee. Organisations like...

item.title: Dauphin Hospital Closure Due to Flooding Sparks Calls for Temporary Field Hospital
item.description: Residents of Dauphin, Manitoba, are urgently calling for a temporary field hospital after the closure of their regional health-care centre due to severe flooding. The Dauphin Regional Health Centre wa...

item.title: US Fans Prank World Cup Stars with Declaration of Independence Signing at Training Base
item.description: The article highlights a series of events and stories surrounding the 2026 FIFA World Cup, focusing on fan interactions, player activities, and unexpected moments. It begins with a prank where US fans...

item.title: Kenya Govt Funds 122K Youth Entrepreneurs Under NYOTA Program
item.description: The Kenyan government has released over Sh3 billion under the second phase of the National Youth Opportunities Towards Advancement (NYOTA) program, supporting more than 122,000 young entrepreneurs nat...

item.title: KP Governor Warns Against Expanding MP Privileges Amid Austerity
item.description: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Governor Faisal Karim Kundi has expressed concerns about a new law expanding the powers and immunities of provincial assembly members, emphasizing that public funds should serve the...

item.title: Car driver nabbed in Kerala fatal accident after CCTV evidence reveals hit-and-run incident
item.description: Two IT professionals, Naveen Sibi and Mridula Ann Umma, were killed in a fatal accident in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, after a car struck their motorcycle from behind. Initial reports suggested a self...

item.title: Volkswagen's Restructuring: Job Cuts and Production Overhaul in Europe
item.description: Volkswagen, Europe's largest automaker, is undergoing a major restructuring as it faces unprecedented pressure to restructure its business model. The company plans to cut its model lineup by up to hal...

item.title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives at British Museum after 1,000-year absence
item.description: The Bayeux Tapestry, a 11th-century embroidery depicting the events leading to the Norman Conquest of England in 1066, has arrived at the British Museum after a secretive journey from northern France....

item.title: Kenya's National Assembly Challenges High Court Ruling on Cabinet Gender Composition
item.description: The Kenyan National Assembly has officially appealed a High Court ruling that deemed President William Ruto's Cabinet unconstitutional due to non-compliance with the two-thirds gender principle in Art...

item.title: [OPINION] Impeachment Trials Need Political Judgment Over Legal Formalities
item.description: The article critiques the overemphasis on legal procedures in impeachment trials, arguing that public trust and political judgment should take precedence. It highlights how lawyers' focus on courtroom...

item.title: NASCO Challenges Court Ruling, Cautions Against Land Transactions
item.description: NASCO Town Limited has appealed the Lagos State High Court's decision that voided the Federal Government's acquisition of 292 hectares of land. The company warned the public against any transactions i...

item.title: Iran mourns Khamenei’s death as succession crisis and US tensions persist
item.description: Iran’s late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was buried in Mashhad’s holiest shrine following a week of nationwide mourning and protests. His son, Mojtaba Khamenei, declared his succession by c...

item.title: Nigeria's Capital Market Gains Recognition for 2027 Frontier Market Status
item.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices has placed Nigeria on its 2027 Country Classification Watchlist, signaling potential reclassification from a Standalone Market to a Frontier Market. This move highlights improvem...

item.title: Delhi to Introduce Electric Vans for Improved Connectivity in Congested Areas
item.description: The Delhi government is exploring plans to introduce seven-seater electric vans to enhance last-mile connectivity in areas where conventional buses cannot operate. Officials from the transport departm...

item.title: Tanzania's Alcohol Sector Growth and President Mkapa's Economic Legacy
item.description: Over the past three decades, Tanzania's alcohol sector has transformed from a state-controlled monopoly into a thriving industry, driven by economic reforms initiated by former President Benjamin Mkap...

item.title: Lance Gokongwei Joins PhilWeb Board After P2 Billion Investment
item.description: Tycoon Lance Gokongwei has joined the board of PhilWeb Corporation after investing more than P2 billion in the gaming technology provider. His appointment as a director expands the Gokongwei family's ...

item.title: Cargo plane crash investigation continues as wreckage is recovered from Arabian Sea
item.description: The Pakistan Aviation Authority (PAA) confirmed that the search for the crew of a cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea is ongoing. Additional wreckage of the aircraft, which disappeared from ...

item.title: U.S. Explosive Ordnance Disposal Team Trains with Royal Thai Navy in Joint Exercise
item.description: The article details a joint military training exercise between the U.S. Explosive Ordnance Disposal Mobile Unit (EODMU) 5 and the Royal Thai Navy during CARAT Thailand 2026, a multinational maritime s...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Education Infrastructure Project with Barrick Twiga Minerals Partnership
item.description: The Tanzanian government, in partnership with Barrick Twiga Minerals, has initiated a major education infrastructure project aimed at improving learning conditions across mainland Tanzania. The initia...

item.title: New Book Releases Highlight Diverse Themes and Authors
item.description: The article showcases a curated list of newly released books across various genres, emphasizing their unique themes and author backgrounds. 'How To Be Happy' by Declan Edwards explores sustainable hap...
